nameMeanings.name: Say
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sey
meanings: English and Scottish: probably a variant of   Say   particularly prevalent in Scotland.
Dutch and Flemish: from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Sigiheri (see Seger ).
West African (Ghana): Akan (Fante) name of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Sayer
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Saye
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
meanings: Hebrew: Bitter; Marion is the French form.

variants.name: Marion
meanings: Originally a medieval French diminutive form of Marie , introduced to Britain in the Middle Ages. In some places it was taken as a pet form of Margaret or Margery . As a male name, it is an altered form of the Continental male name Marian, a derivative of Latin Marianus (from Marius ), but is now rarely, if ever, used. It was the birth name of the American film star John Wayne ( 1907–79 ).

variants.name: Marcia
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Say was born about 1280, in Kent, England, United Kingdom, her father, William de Saye IV, was 28 and her mother, Elizabeth, was 24. She had at least 1 daughter with Sir John Northwode II. She died about 1320, in her hometown, at the age of 42.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Mary Say est née environ 1280, à Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William de Saye IV, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth, avait 24 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Sir John Northwode II. Elle est décédée environ 1320, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 42 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Mary Say um 1280 in Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William de Saye IV 28 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth 24. Sie hatte mit Sir John Northwode II mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb um 1320 im Alter von 42 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Mary Say è nata il circa 1280 nel Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William de Saye IV, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth, aveva 24 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Sir John Northwode II. Morì circa 1320, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 42 anni.
